calendar.matters:
FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Mar-12-2018 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Dec-04-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Sterling Ross for and with Caron Schmierer.

Ms. Schmierer stated that the check issued to Fiduciary Services was an error, that it should not have been done, that the money was put in a non-interest bearing a checking account at the Bank of Marin, that the money was used extensively for the use of Ms. Owens and that it was her responsibility. Mr. Ross addressed the request for Attorney's fees stating that it hopes the Court read the Conservator's response to the Examiner's notes, that the supplement provided time itemization and that there is nothing more he can submit. Mr. Ross also stated that there is no statute for the Trust to be brought under Court supervision when the same person is the Conservator and the Trustee of the Trust, and that they would like to have authority to deal with the property in Ireland as it is in the Special Needs Trust. The Second and Final Account and Report of Trustee, etc. is taken under submission. The Court advised Counsel that it will probably take out the property issue.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Oct-31-2017 Continued To Nov-14-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FIRST ACCOUNTING OF TRUSTEE
Off Calendar May-17-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Sterling Ross for and with (trustee) Caron Schmierer.

Mr. Ross reported that this was originally a living trustee, that Andree is the sister and is conserved, that there are no assets in the conservatorship, that the status report was filed one day late and subsequently they were told to bring the trust under court supervision, that there is no reason to bring the trust under court supervision, that they objected but the court denied the objection and ordered that an accounting be filed, that they submitted it, that the probate examiner has had issues with it, that they have filed a supplemental petition and declaration to deal with the issues and that they are not trying to hide anything. The Court asked if there were additional itemized statements. Mr. Ross stated that they have provided time records and billing statements, that a lot of the work was in regards to the trust administration and that items were paid without a court order because the trust was not under court supervision at that time. Ms. Schmierer stated that she had hoped the declaration would shed light on the complexities of the case, that she was able to get deferrals on payments and that without that there would be no money for Andree. Mr. Ross stated that Ms. Schmierer has done a wonderful job, that payment of attorney's fees are not paid by public benefits and have to be paid out of the special needs trust. The Court stated that there is frustration on peoples perspectives on what is required. Ms. Schmierer stated that she was hoping that with the narrative the probate examiner would understand the timeline and what happened with the expenditures, that it would give a background and that on Saturday she reviewed the inventory of what is in the storage unit and can probably close it out by 6/1/16. The Court confirmed that no fees were paid by the parent trust. Mr. Ross stated that they provided timesheets and background for everything paid including the time that the trust was not under court supervision and that the fee request is for work not previously paid. The Court confirmed that there are no additional timesheets. The Court took the accounting petition under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FIRST ACCOUNTING OF TRUSTEE
